name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: The right to petition...
body:
[Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501129758%2501100238%2b-%2b06-30-2017%2bFourth%2bof%2bJuly%250114286.5848052.3438288.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501129758%2501100238%2b-%2b06-30-2017%2bFourth%2bof%2bJuly%250114286.5848052.3438288.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
| |
---
****
| | [](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239) | **July 2, 2017** |
---|---|---|---
| | | | | | | As we gear up to celebrate the 4th of July, it's a good time to remind ourselves of the values that make America special. One of our core principles is the right of free expression and the right to petition our government. This is a moment when it is more important than ever for citizens to use the power of their voices to be heard in the halls of Congress on a range of vital issues, especially the future of health care in America. We should be working to improve our health care system, not take a U-turn backwards. Americans have been speaking up across Maryland and the nation to let their Senators and Representatives know that they don't want to dismantle our health care system and hurt vulnerable Americans in order to give tax cuts to the ultra-wealthy. As a result of those grassroots efforts, Americans have succeeded in delaying a vote on the dangerous Senate Republican "Wealth Care" bill. But as Yogi Berra once said, "It ain't over till it's over." Now is the time to redouble our efforts to stop this immoral bill and work together to improve our health care system, like reducing the costs of prescription drugs. This week Americans will have a chance to see their Senators and Representatives at parades, picnics, and barbecues. It is my hope that, after talking with their constituents, every Member of Congress will come back to Washington ready to work on a bipartisan basis to improve our health care system. Americans want to make health care accessible and affordable to more people - not fewer. Over the last few weeks I've heard powerful stories from Marylanders about how the Affordable Care Act has helped save their lives or those of their loved ones. In the last week, I have received over 6,000 calls and emails from Marylanders opposed to the Senate Republican plan that would leave 22 million more Americans without insurance and harm children, working families, and seniors all across Maryland - all to give the very wealthy a windfall take break. I'm thankful for every single Marylander, regardless of their views, who has reached out to tell their story and express their opinion about health care and many other issues. As we celebrate our freedom this 4th of July, your voices are more important than ever - and they are truly having an impact. Have a safe and happy 4th of July. Chris Van Hollen
United States Senator **Rallying for Health Care in Bowie** Thank you to the more than 500 Marylanders who came out to our Health Care Forum at the Greater Mt. Nebo AME Church in Bowie last weekend. With the help of the Collective Empowerment Group, faith leaders, and experts like Baltimore's Health Commissioner, Dr. Leana Wen, we discussed the moral imperative to provide quality, affordable health care to all Americans and offered community members wellness and insurance enrollment resources. **Fighting for Jobs Across Maryland** **** As member of the Appropriations Committee, I recently joined a group of Senators representing six states [voicing support](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d14328.5669291.3438427.7135194%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fcontent%252fcardin-van-hollen-urge-appropriators-maintain-critical-funding-appalachian-regional%26redir_log%3d23062958466879&redir_log=833124915942065) to fully fund the Appalachian Regional Commission (ARC). ARC supports job-creating community projects across the 13 Appalachian states. It has funded many projects that create jobs and economic opportunity in Western Maryland, including recent grants for [STEM education at Allegany College](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d14328.5669291.3438427.7135194%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fcontent%252fcardin-van-hollen-announce-67300-grant-allegany-college-stem-enhancement-project%26redir_log%3d197782255044843&redir_log=321508789362027), [business infrastructure in Garrett County](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d14328.5669291.3438427.7135194%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fcontent%252fvan-hollen-cardin-announce-750000-arc-grant-garrett-county%26redir_log%3d36537133689682&redir_log=694464152641859), [and water infrastructure in Boonsboro](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2foutreach.senate.gov%2fiqextranet%2fiqClickTrk.aspx%3f%26cid%3dSenVanHollen%26crop%3d14328.5669291.3438427.7135194%26report_id%3d%26redirect%3dhttps%253a%252f%252fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%252fcontent%252fvan-hollen-cardin-announce-120000-arc-grant-boonsboro-sewer-replacement%26redir_log%3d040861216315777&redir_log=453599143971958). ARC is a great example of a state-federal partnership that fosters economic opportunity in areas that need it the most - but President Trump's budget completely eliminates the program. I will fight that effort tooth and nail to protect jobs and economic growth in Maryland. ******Serving Marylanders - Offices Open Throughout Our State** **** I'm pleased to announce the grand opening of our Baltimore Regional Office, our hub for community outreach and constituent services in the Baltimore region. The Baltimore office and our offices throughout the state are a resource for Marylanders to seek help with any constituent issues they make have, whether it involves assistance with Social Security, veterans care, Medicare coverage, or many other matters. In addition to the Baltimore office, we have offices open throughout the state in Hagerstown, Annapolis, and Rockville, and offices will be opening soon in Cambridge and Largo. Full contact information for all office locations is below. No Marylander's issue or concern is too small. And if it's a matter we can't solve by ourselves, we will reach out to our partners in the non-profit or private sector, as well as those at all levels of government: federal, state and local. Even when the Congress seems to be gridlocked, our work in the community continues. My team and I will always be there to listen and assist.
---

| | **Washington, DC Office**
110 Hart Senate Office Building
Washington, DC 20510
Phone: (202) 224-4654
Fax: (202) 228-0629
TTY: (202) 224-1546 **Western Maryland Office**
** **32 West Washington Street, Suite 203
Hagerstown, MD 21740
Phone: (301) 797-2826 | **Montgomery County Office**
111 Rockville Pike, Suite 960
Rockville, MD 20850
Phone: (301) 545-1500
Fax: (301) 545-1512 **Eastern Shore Office
(opening soon)
**204 Cedar Street, Suite 200C
Cambridge, MD 21613 **Prince George 's County Office
(opening soon)
**1101 Mercantile Lane, Suite 210
Largo, MD 20774 [Please click here to unsubscribe](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//EsurveyForm.aspx?__cid=SenVanHollen&__sid=100007&__crop=14286.5848052.3438288.7517223) | **Baltimore Office**
1900 North Howard Street, Suite 100
Baltimore, MD 21218
Phone: (667) 212-4610 **Anne Arundel and Southern Maryland Office:
**60 West Street, Suite 107
Annapolis, MD 21401
Phone: (410) 263-1325 | |

name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: 3 minute read
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4de8/4665504/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4de8/4665504/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hey there --
Last year, we put everything we had into getting Maggie Hassan elected to the Senate because we knew that she'd fight for people like us and families like ours, not corporate special interests.
Senator Hassan is following through. Affordable health care and Medicaid are on the line, and she's making it a top priority to ensure that hard-working families can continue to access the care they need to get and stay healthy.
[**That's why we wanted to make sure you saw this recent TIME article featuring Senator Hassan and the importance of protecting access to affordable care, not taking it away.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4de9/4665504/VEsF/ "http://time.com/4838695/beyond-repeal-and-replace/")
In it, Granite Stater Ashley Hurteau tells her story about her heroin addiction and how because of Medicaid expansion she's now in recovery.
You'll also read why the fight to protect health care is a personal one for Senator Hassan, and why she's working so hard to make sure that all of us have access to the same support that her family has had.
[**We know that you're probably busy this holiday weekend, but we hope you'll take a few minutes to read it before heading off to a BBQ, parade, or whatever festivities you have planned.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4de9/4665504/VEsC/ "http://time.com/4838695/beyond-repeal-and-replace/")
Thanks for taking the time, and have a fun and safe Fourth of July.
-- All of us on Team Maggie
P.S. -- Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump are likely to bring this bill to a vote in the next few weeks. Senator Hassan and her Democratic colleagues in the Senate are fighting day and night to keep it from passing, but they need your support. [**If you're able, donate $3 or more now to help protect access to affordable health care.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4dea/4665504/VEsD/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxNzA2X3NlbmF0ZV9oZWFsdGhjYXJlIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxNzA3MDFfVElNRV9hY3QiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/201706_senate_healthcare?refcode=e20170701_TIME_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")

[Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4dea/4665504/VEsA/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxNzA2X3NlbmF0ZV9oZWFsdGhjYXJlIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlMjAxNzA3MDFfVElNRV9hY3QiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/201706_senate_healthcare?refcode=e20170701_TIME_act&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
To unsubscribe from the Maggie for NH email list, please click here: [Unsubscribe](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdefb/2a380d31/19d4d5d5/fe4deb/4665504/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

name: Team Kasich
subject: Celebrating our Freedoms
body:
We're wishing you and your family a safe and enjoyable
Independence Day celebration this week.

Peter, we're wishing you and
your family a safe and enjoyable Independence Day celebration
this week.

No matter the challenges we face
together, we remain unified in celebration of the freedoms that
make America great. Happy Independence Day!

-Team Kasich

P.S. In case you missed it, Gov.
Kasich was a guest on ABC's 'This Week' to discuss what can be
accomplished when our leaders put the country before party
loyalty and politics. Watch the interview (here).

Paid for by Kasich
for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.
www.JohnKasich.com
